content.text:
[{"id":"bb0aaef7-6adc-4e5b-8835-9bb6d75c3251","body":"## 🧪 QA Test Report — FRG-184
**Tested against:** https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app  
**Test suite:** https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa (branch: main)  
**Run date:** 2026-03-07

---

### Test Results Summary

| Tier | Tests | Passed | Failed |
|------|-------|--------|--------|
| Smoke | 8 | 8 | 0 |
| Regression | 18 | 18 | 0 |
| Critical | 5 | 5 | 0 |
| **Total** | **31** | **31** | **0** |

> Note: The bug documentation test (`[BUG FRG-184]`) is marked `test.fail()` — it passes by confirming the known defect is present.

---

### ✅ What's Working

**LLM Decisions (Grok/Claude via `/api/llm`)**
- All 3 characters (Maple, Sol, Nyx) fire POST requests to `/api/llm` on page load
- Requests include character name, tile position, mood/energy, and nearby object context
- Responses return valid JSON with `action`, `targetId`/`targetTile`, `dialogue`, `description`
- Each character respects the 30s decision interval (only 1 request per char in first 10s)
- All API calls return HTTP 200 — no server errors
- AI dialogue is stored on characters (`currentDialogue`) and appears in console logs

**A\* Pathfinding / Movement**
- Characters enter `walking` state within 15 seconds of load
- Walking characters' tile coordinates change over time (confirmed movement)
- Characters stay within walkable bounds (cols 1–17, rows 2–9) — no out-of-bounds observed
- State machine transitions correctly: `idle → walking → idle`

**Object Interactions (state changes visible)**
- Objects change state within 60 seconds (observed: stove→cooking, kotatsu→cozy, tv→on, bonsai→tending)
- Debug panel reflects live state changes in real time
- Debug panel "use" buttons correctly trigger state transitions
- Object auto-transitions back to idle after their configured duration (tested with bonsai: 12s)
- Object states are visible in the debug overlay panel

**Speech Bubbles**
- Preset dialogue lines are loaded for all 3 characters
- AI-generated dialogue is stored on characters after LLM response
- `pendingSpeech` setter is triggered within 20–40 real seconds (confirmed via property interception)
- Speech text is non-empty strings from the character's preset dialogue pool

**Sim Clock**
- Sim time advances in real time (simTimeMs increases)
- Clock format is correct (`H:MM AM/PM`)
- simSpeed=60 confirmed (clock runs faster than real time in production)

---

### 🐛 Bug Found — BLOCKS Acceptance

**`AIEngine` `interact` decisions never trigger object state changes**

**Severity:** High — core acceptance criterion ("interact with objects, state changes visible") is only partially met via *random wander*, not via AI decisions.

**Location:** `src/AIEngine.js` → `_applyDecision()`, `interact` case

**Root Cause:**
```js
// AIEngine._applyDecision (CURRENT — BROKEN)
case 'interact': {
  const obj = objects.find(o => o.id === decision.targetId);
  if (obj) {
    character.moveTo(obj.tx, obj.ty);       // ❌ walks TO object tile (may not be walkable)
    character._pendingInteract = obj.id;    // ❌ sets _pendingInteract (wrong property)
  }
  break;
}
```

```js
// Engine.js — SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival
// Only triggers when char.targetObjectId is set (never set by AI)
if (char.action === 'idle' && char.targetObjectId) {
    this._handleObjectArrival(char);        // ❌ never called for AI decisions
}
```

**Evidence (confirmed in 3 consecutive test runs):**
```json
[
  { "label": "Maple", "pendingInteract": "tea_set", "targetObjectId": null },
  { "label": "Sol",   "pendingInteract": "tea_set", "targetObjectId": null },
  { "label": "Nyx",   "pendingInteract": "bonsai",  "targetObjectId": null }
]
```
All 3 characters had `_pendingInteract` set from AI decision, but `targetObjectId` was null — so `_handleObjectArrival` never fires.

**Suggested Fix in `AIEngine._applyDecision`:**
```js
case 'interact': {
  const obj = objects.find(o => o.id === decision.targetId);
  if (obj) {
    // Walk ADJACENT to the object (same pattern as wander engine)
    const adjTile = engine._findAdjacentWalkable(obj.tx, obj.ty);
    if (adjTile && character.moveTo(adjTile.tx, adjTile.ty)) {
      character.targetObjectId = obj.id;   // ✅ correct property
      character.pendingAction  = 'use';
    }
  }
  break;
}
```
Note: `_findAdjacentWalkable` is on `SimulationEngine`, so either make it accessible to AIEngine or pass it as a helper.

---

### Additional Observations

- **favicon.ico 404** — minor, no functional impact
- **Random wander still works** — object state changes DO occur via the 30% random-wander path in `SimulationEngine._doWander`, so the sim isn't completely static. This masks the AI interact bug visually.
- **Speech bubbles timing** — `pendingSpeech` is set and cleared in the same RAF frame; tests required property-setter interception to reliably catch it. This is working correctly in production.

---

### Recommendation: ⛔ BLOCK — Do Not Merge/Close

The AI sim loop is partially working:
- ✅ LLM queries fire correctly
- ✅ Characters move via A* 
- ✅ Speech/dialogue system works
- ✅ Object states change (via random wander)
- ❌ **AI `interact` decisions never trigger object state transitions** — the core AC "characters interact with objects (state changes visible)" is not fulfilled via AI decisions, only via random wander fallback

Please fix `AIEngine._applyDecision` to set `targetObjectId` (not `_pendingInteract`) and walk to an adjacent tile. After the fix, remove `test.fail()` from the critical bug test — it will then act as a passing regression test.
","createdAt":"2026-03-06T18:18:37.422Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-06T18:18:37.401Z","author":{"id":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","name":"Chill"}},{"id":"67e50aa9-3a5a-4d55-9aba-fb120fefcb14","body":"**Assessment:**

The sim engine runs — characters wander, interact with objects (30% chance), speech bubbles fire, object state machines tick, sim clock advances. All working on the live site.

**Gap:** AIEngine exists but is disabled (commented out in scene.js). To enable LLM decisions:
1. Need a server-side proxy (Vercel serverless fn) — current code makes client-side fetch, which would expose the API key
2. Need an API key for xAI/Grok or Anthropic
3. Need Kim to decide which LLM to use

**Proposed next step:** Create a /api/decide endpoint on Vercel that proxies LLM calls, then wire AIEngine to use it.","createdAt":"2026-03-04T13:02:23.363Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-04T13:02:23.340Z","author":{"id":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","name":"Chill"}}]

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me look at the relevant source files to understand the codebase before making changes.

The bug is in `src/AIEngine.js` → `_applyDecision()`, `interact` case. The fix is to:
1. Use `character.targetObjectId = obj.id` instead of `character._pendingInteract = obj.id`
2. Walk to adjacent walkable tile instead of the object tile directly (using `engine._findAdjacentWalkable`)

Let me explore the codebase.

content.text: Now I have the full context. Let me explore the relevant source files.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue clearly:

1. In `AIEngine._applyDecision`, the `interact` case:
   - Currently: calls `character.moveTo(obj.tx, obj.ty)` (walks to object tile, may not be walkable)
   - Currently: sets `character._pendingInteract = obj.id` (wrong property)
   
2. The fix needed:
   - Use `engine._findAdjacentWalkable(obj.tx, obj.ty)` to find adjacent walkable tile
   - Walk to that adjacent tile instead
   - Set `character.targetObjectId = obj.id` (correct property)
   - Set `character.pendingAction = 'use'`

The problem is that `AIEngine._applyDecision` doesn't have direct access to the engine instance to call `_findAdjacentWalkable`, so I need to figure out how to pass the engine reference or restructure the code to make this work.

Looking at how `AIEngine.update` gets called from the main engine, it receives `deltaMs`, the characters array, all objects, and a `simState` object with metadata like `timeString`. I could extend that `simState` to include the engine reference or the `_findAdjacentWalkable` method itself.

The cleanest approach without major refactoring would be to pass the `findAdjacentWalkable` function through `simState` when calling `update`. That way `AIEngine` can access it as `simState.findAdjacentWalkable` without needing a direct reference to the engine.

Looking at the call chain, I need to thread `simState` through `_requestDecision` and into `_applyDecision` so the adjacency check is available where the decision gets applied. I see the issue now—`simState` is being passed to `_requestDecision` but then dropped before reaching `_applyDecision`, so I can't access `findAdjacentWalkable` where I need it. The fix is straightforward: thread `simState` through to `_applyDecision`, add the helper function to simState in Engine.js, and then use it to find walkable adjacent tiles. Finishing the interact case logic...

Now I'm ready to create the feature branch and implement these changes. The QA team mentioned removing the `test.fail()` from their regression test once this is fixed, but since those tests live in a separate repo, I'll focus on getting this code change in place.

content.text: I have a clear understanding of the bug and fix. Let me create the feature branch and implement the fix.

content.thinking:
Now let me implement the fix. There are two files to change:
1. `Engine.js` - add `findAdjacentWalkable` to the simState passed to aiEngine.update
2. `AIEngine.js` - fix the interact case to use `targetObjectId`, walk to adjacent tile, and pass simState through

Let me make both changes.

content.text:
Now implement the two-part fix:

**1. `Engine.js` — expose `findAdjacentWalkable` via simState**

input.command:
git add src/AIEngine.js src/Engine.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: set targetObjectId and walk to adjacent tile in AIEngine interact (FRG-190)

AIEngine._applyDecision was setting _pendingInteract (nonexistent property)
and walking to the object tile directly. SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival
only fires when targetObjectId is set, so AI interact decisions never triggered
object state changes.

Fix:
- Pass findAdjacentWalkable helper via simState from SimulationEngine.update
- Walk to adjacent walkable tile (matching the wander engine pattern)
- Set character.targetObjectId and character.pendingAction = 'use'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: set targetObjectId and walk to adjacent tile in AIEngine interact (FRG-190)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- **Bug:** `AIEngine._applyDecision` set `character._pendingInteract` (nonexistent/ignored property) and called `moveTo(obj.tx, obj.ty)` (walks onto the non-walkable object tile). `SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival` only fires when `char.targetObjectId` is set — so AI `interact` decisions never triggered object state changes.
- **Fix:** Walk to an adjacent walkable tile via `simState.findAdjacentWalkable` (same helper the wander engine uses), then set `character.targetObjectId` and `character.pendingAction = 'use'`.
- **Plumbing:** `SimulationEngine.update` now passes `findAdjacentWalkable: this._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(this)` in the simState object so `AIEngine` can use it without holding a direct engine reference.

Fixes: FRG-190 | Related: FRG-184

## Test plan
- [ ] Deploy to staging and confirm AI `interact` decisions now walk characters to adjacent tiles and trigger object state transitions (e.g. stove→cooking, bonsai→tending)
- [ ] Confirm `targetObjectId` is no longer null after an AI interact decision fires (was confirmed null in QA evidence on FRG-184)
- [ ] Re-run QA suite from FRG-184; remove `test.fail()` from the critical bug regression test — it should now pass cleanly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

input.body:
## Implementation Summary — FRG-190

**Root cause confirmed:** `AIEngine._applyDecision` had two bugs in the `interact` case:
1. Called `character.moveTo(obj.tx, obj.ty)` — walks onto the object tile itself, which is not walkable
2. Set `character._pendingInteract = obj.id` — a nonexistent property; `SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival` checks `char.targetObjectId`, so it never fired

**Fix applied (2 files):**

- **`src/AIEngine.js`** — Rewrote the `interact` case to call `simState.findAdjacentWalkable(obj.tx, obj.ty)` and, on success, set `character.targetObjectId = obj.id` and `character.pendingAction = 'use'` — matching the exact pattern used by the wander engine's 30% object-visit path.
- **`src/Engine.js`** — Added `findAdjacentWalkable: this._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(this)` to the simState object passed to `aiEngine.update`, exposing the helper without giving AIEngine a direct engine reference.

**PR:** https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/1

**Next step for QA:** After merging, re-run the FRG-184 test suite and remove `test.fail()` from the critical bug regression test — it should now pass as a live regression guard.

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `src/AIEngine.js` — Fixed `_applyDecision` interact case: replaced `_pendingInteract` (wrong property) with `targetObjectId`/`pendingAction`, replaced direct object-tile walk with adjacent-walkable-tile walk; threaded `simState` through to `_applyDecision`
- `src/Engine.js` — Added `findAdjacentWalkable: this._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(this)` to the simState passed to `aiEngine.update`

## Key Decisions
- Exposed `_findAdjacentWalkable` via the existing `simState` parameter rather than giving `AIEngine` a direct reference to `SimulationEngine` — keeps the coupling minimal and matches how `timeString` was already shared
- Used optional chaining (`simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.()`) so the call degrades safely if simState is missing the helper (e.g. in unit tests)

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/frg-190-fix-aiengine-interact-decisions`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/1

- `src/AIEngine.js` — Fixed `_applyDecision` interact case: replaced `_pendingInteract` (wrong property) with `targetObjectId`/`pendingAction`, replaced direct object-tile walk with adjacent-walkable-tile walk; threaded `simState` through to `_applyDecision`
- `src/Engine.js` — Added `findAdjacentWalkable: this._findAdjacentWalkable.bind(this)` to the simState passed to `aiEngine.update`

## Key Decisions
- Exposed `_findAdjacentWalkable` via the existing `simState` parameter rather than giving `AIEngine` a direct reference to `SimulationEngine` — keeps the coupling minimal and matches how `timeString` was already shared
- Used optional chaining (`simState?.findAdjacentWalkable?.()`) so the call degrades safely if simState is missing the helper (e.g. in unit tests)
